_license: Original content for Open Dungeon Master. Suggested opening spell lists for players who do not know 5e. These are editorial recommendations plus one line on why each pick is useful; no publisher's text is reproduced.
_note: A new player facing an empty search box does not know that a druid wants Goodberry or that a wizard wants Shield, so they pick nothing and their character is weaker for it. The builder offers these as one-tap picks alongside the full searchable list, and nothing here is forced: they are a starting point, not a restriction.
_shape: classes[classId] = { why: one line on how the class casts, cantrips: [{ n: name, d: why it is a good pick }], spells: [...] }. Custom genre casters borrow a list through spellClassFor().

bard.why: You know a small fixed list of spells and cast with Charisma. Pick a mix of one good damage option and several ways to control a scene.

cantrips.d: Damage plus disadvantage on their next attack, at range, for free.
The most flexible problem-solver in the game once you get used to it.
Small magical tricks that solve small problems constantly.

spells.d: A bonus action that gets a downed ally back on their feet from 60 feet away.
Advantage on every attack against everything caught in it. Excellent with a big party.
Strong damage that also forces the target to run away from you.
Ends an early fight outright against weak enemies.

cleric.why: You prepare spells fresh after each long rest from the whole cleric list, so you can adapt daily. Wisdom is your casting ability.

cantrips.d: Reliable ranged damage that ignores cover well.
A free d4 on an ally's ability check. Use it constantly outside combat.
Booming voice and small omens; surprisingly persuasive.

spells.d: The straightforward heal when someone needs real hit points back.
A bonus action revive at range. Often better than Cure Wounds in a fight.
Adds a d4 to attacks and saves for three allies. One of the best low-level spells there is.
Big radiant damage and the next attack against the target has advantage.

druid.why: You prepare spells fresh after each long rest from the whole druid list. Wisdom is your casting ability, and you also have Wild Shape.

cantrips.d: A light source and a ranged attack in one.
Lets you fight with your club using Wisdom, so you need no Strength.
A free d4 on an ally's ability check, over and over.

spells.d: Ten berries that each heal 1 hit point and feed someone for a day. Quietly one of the best 1st-level spells.
Restrains a group of enemies in a 20-foot square.
Your direct heal.
Advantage on attacks against everything it catches.

paladin.why: You prepare a small list from the paladin spells and cast with Charisma. Most of your damage still comes from your weapon and Divine Smite.

spells.d: A d4 on attacks and saves for three allies. Almost always your best use of a slot.
Healing on top of Lay on Hands.
A bonus action for +2 AC on you or an ally.
Pins one dangerous enemy on you and away from the squishy party members.

ranger.why: You know a small fixed list and cast with Wisdom. Your spells mostly sharpen your weapon attacks and your travel.

spells.d: An extra 1d6 on every hit against your target, all day. Take this first.
The party's backup healing.
Cheap healing and food that solves survival problems.
A reaction that halves an elemental hit and adds damage to your next attack.

sorcerer.why: You know a very small fixed list, so pick versatile spells. Charisma is your casting ability, and Metamagic bends what you cast.

cantrips.d: Your reliable at-will damage at long range.
Endless small utility.
Distraction, cover and misdirection for free.
Handles things you would rather not touch yourself.

spells.d: A reaction for +5 AC that turns a hit into a miss. Never a wasted slot.
Damage that simply cannot miss.
Your big single-target hit, in whatever damage type the fight needs.

warlock.why: You have very few slots but they come back on a short rest and always cast at their highest level. Charisma is your casting ability.

cantrips.d: The best attack cantrip in the game and the core of most warlocks. Take it.
Flexible trickery that costs nothing.

spells.d: Extra damage on every hit plus disadvantage on one ability. Pairs perfectly with Eldritch Blast.
Temporary hit points that hurt anyone who hits you, and it scales beautifully with your slots.
A reaction that punishes whoever just damaged you.

wizard.why: You have the largest spell list in the game and prepare fresh each day from your spellbook. Intelligence is your casting ability.

cantrips.d: Your reliable at-will damage.
A remote hand for traps, levers and pockets.
The utility cantrip you will use most often.
Solves more problems than it has any right to.

spells.d: A reaction for +5 AC. The single best 1st-level wizard spell.
Damage that always lands.
Can end a fight before it starts at low level.
A ritual, so it costs you nothing to cast, and it answers constant questions.
AC 13 plus Dexterity all day if you wear no armor.

artificer.why: You prepare a small list and cast with Intelligence, using your tools as a focus. Your infusions matter as much as your spells.

cantrips.d: A free d4 on ability checks, all day.
Repairs objects, which comes up more than you would think.

spells.d: You are often the party's only healer.
Advantage on attacks against everything it catches.
A reaction that halves elemental damage.
